titleInfo.title: Imperial Tombs in Tang China, 618–907: The Politics of Paradise

mods.note: p. 15: It is interesting to note the Buddhist content in the sculptural programme at the site, with a stone relief in the form of a niche occupied by a seated Buddha flanked by attendant bodhisattvas, and donor figures. Today, the condition of the relief is poor and the loss of detail prevents identification of the figures, although it is likely that the primary figure was Maitreya or Amitabha. The image recalls those in niches at Longmen,... p. 107: This is the case in the Northern Qi tomb of the high official Lou Rui, dating from 570, for example, where journey scenes were portrayed on the east and west walls of the 21 m long passage...

titleInfo.title: RoutledgeCurzon Studies in the Early History of Asia
